title: Taupo in June
meta_desc: Discover Taupo in June with kids, enjoy Huka Falls, Lake Taupo, and thermal pools with a budget of $200-350/day for a family of 4, suitable for all ages
intro: Taupo is a great mid-island stop, offering a mix of free and paid activities for families, including the spectacular Huka Falls and Lake Taupo. In June, the weather is cool, with average highs of 12°C and lows of 2°C, making it perfect for outdoor activities like hiking and swimming in the thermal pools. With a budget of $200-350/day for a family of 4, you can enjoy all that Taupo has to offer.

sections.heading: Weather and What's On
sections.body:
In June, Taupo experiences winter, with cooler temperatures and some rainfall. However, this doesn't stop the fun, as many attractions are indoors or can be enjoyed in the cooler weather.

The Matariki Festival is on in June, celebrating the Maori New Year with cultural events, performances, and fireworks. It's a great time to experience the local culture and community.

sections.heading: Best Activities for June
sections.body:
The Huka Falls are a must-visit, and in June, you can enjoy the scenic walkway and viewing platform without the crowds. The Wairakei Terraces & Thermal Pools are also a great option, with warm water and stunning scenery.

For teens and adults, Skydive Taupo is an adrenaline-pumping activity, with prices starting from $299 per person. The Huka Jet is another exciting option, with prices from $89 per person.

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Booking Ahead
sections.body:
In June, the crowds are smaller compared to the peak summer months, making it easier to enjoy the attractions without long wait times. However, it's still a good idea to book ahead for popular activities like Skydive Taupo and the Huka Jet.

The AC Baths and Wairakei Terraces & Thermal Pools can get busy on weekends and school holidays, so it's a good idea to plan your visit during the week if possible.

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
list: Pack warm clothing, including jackets and gloves, as it can get cool in the mornings and evenings
Bring a picnic lunch to enjoy at one of the many parks or scenic spots
Take advantage of the free activities, such as the Huka Falls viewing platform and walkway, and the Spa Thermal Park outdoor hot springs
Book a family-friendly accommodation that offers amenities like a kitchen and laundry facilities
Check the weather forecast before heading out and plan your activities accordingly
